title: Early vs Late Binding Demo
prompt_md:
## Goal
Write a program that demonstrates both early binding (explicit scope-resolution call) and late binding (virtual dispatch through a pointer) on the same method.

## Input
No input — all data is hardcoded.

## Output
Print exactly three lines:
```
Base::greet
Base::greet
Derived::greet
```

## Requirements
1. `Base` has `virtual void greet() const` printing `Base::greet`.
2. `Derived` inherits from `Base` and overrides `greet()` printing `Derived::greet`.
3. In `main`, create a `Derived` object `d`.
4. First call: `d.Base::greet()` — explicit early binding, must print `Base::greet`.
5. Second call: through a `Base*` set to `&d`, call `Base::greet()` explicitly (scope resolution) — early binding, prints `Base::greet`.
6. Third call: through a `Base*` set to `&d`, call `greet()` without scope resolution — late binding, prints `Derived::greet`.

title: Extend Base Behavior in Override
prompt_md:
## Goal
Fix the bug in the program below. It should print `Base log` then `Derived action` but prints only `Derived action`.

## Input
No input.

## Output
```
Base log
Derived action
```

## Requirements
1. `Logger` has `virtual void run() const` that prints `Base log`.
2. `Task` overrides `run()` and should first call `Logger::run()` explicitly (early binding), then print `Derived action`.
3. The bug is that the explicit base call `Logger::run()` is missing from `Task::run()`.
4. Fix by adding the explicit early-binding call at the start of `Task::run()`.
5. Do not change `Logger::run` or `main`.
